title: codonanthus campanulatus
intro: Codonanthus campanulatus, also known as the 'Hairy Button Flower', is a species of flowering plant in the family Goodeniaceae, native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. It is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential antiviral and antifungal properties. In herbal preparations, Codonanthus campanulatus is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
